author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXXIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand, beloved, this Psalm, which I am about to explain, by the grace of God, of our hope in the Lord Jesus Christ, and be of good cheer, because He who promised, will fulfil all, as He has fulfilled much: for it is not our own merit, but His mercy, that gives us confidence in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He Himself is meant, in my belief, by “the understanding of Æthan the Israelite:” which has given this Psalm its title.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You see then, who is meant by Æthan: but the meaning of the word is “strong.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No man in this world is strong, except in the hope of God’s promises: for as to our own deservings, we are weak, in His mercy we are strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Weak then in himself, strong in God’s mercy, the Psalmist thus begins: “I will sing of Thy mercies, O Lord, for ever: with my mouth will I make known Thy truth unto all generations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let my limbs, he saith, serve the Lord: I speak, but it is of Thine I speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my mouth will I make known Thy truth:” if I obey not Thee, I am not Thy servant: if I speak on my own part, I am a liar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To speak then from Thee, and in my own person, are two things: one mine, one Thine: Truth Thine, language mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear then what faithfulness he maketh known, what mercies he singeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast said, Mercy shall be built up for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is this that I sing: this is Thy truth, for the making known of which my mouth serveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such wise Thou sayest, I build, as not to destroy: for some Thou destroyest and buildest not; and some whom Thou destroyest Thou dost rebuild.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless there were some who were destroyed to be rebuilt, Jeremiah would not have written, “See, I have this day set thee to throw down and to build.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed all who formerly worshipped images and stones could not be built up in Christ, without being destroyed as to their old error.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While, unless some were destroyed not to be built up, it would not be written, “He shall destroy them, and not build them up.”… In what follows, he joins these two words, mercy and faithfulness; “For Thou hast said, Mercy shall be built up for ever: Thy truth shall be established in the Heavens:” in which mercy and truth are repeated, “for all the ways of the Lord are mercy and truth,” for truth in the fulfilment of promises could not be shown, unless mercy in the remission of sins preceded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Next, as many things were promised in prophecy even to the people of Israel that came according to the flesh from the seed of Abraham, and that people was increased that the promises of God might be fulfilled in it; while yet God did not close the fountain of His goodness even to the Gentiles, whom He had placed under the rule of the Angels, while He reserved the people of Israel as His own portion: the Apostle expressly mentions the Lord’s mercy and truth as referring to these two parties.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he calls Christ “a minister of the Circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how God deceived not; see how He cast not off His people, whom He foreknew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For while the Apostle is treating of the fall of the Jews, to prevent any from believing them so far disowned of God, that no wheat from that floor’s fanning could reach the granary, he saith, “God hath not cast away His people, whom He foreknew; for I also am an Israelite.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If all that nation are thorns, how am I who speak unto you wheat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that the truth of God was fulfilled in those Israelites who believed, and one wall from the circumcision is thus brought to meet the corner stone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this stone would not form a corner, unless it received another wall from the Gentiles: so that the former wall relates in a special manner to the truth, the latter to the mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Now I say,” says the Apostle, “that Jesus Christ was a minister of the Circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promise made unto the fathers: and that the Gentiles might glorify God for His mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly then is it added, “Thy truth shalt Thou stablish in the Heavens:” for all those Israelites who were called to be Apostles became as Heavens which declare the glory of God: as it is written by them, “The Heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament showeth His handywork.”…Since, although they were taken up from hence before the Church filled the whole world, yet as “their words reached to the ends of the world,” we are right in supposing this which we have just read, “Thy truth shalt Thou stablish in the Heavens,” fulfilled in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast said, I have made a covenant with My chosen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What covenant, but the new, by which we are renewed to a fresh inheritance, in our longing desire and love of which we sing a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have made a covenant with My chosen,” saith the Psalmist: “I have sworn unto David My servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How confidently does he speak, who understands, whose mouth serves truth!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I speak without fear; since “Thou hast said.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If Thou makest me fearless, because Thou hast said, how much more so dost Thou make me, when Thou hast sworn!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the oath of God is the assurance of a promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Man is justly forbidden to swear: lest by the habit of swearing, since a man may be deceived, he fall into perjury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God alone swears securely, because He alone is infallible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see then what God hath sworn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have sworn,” He saith, “to David My servant; thy seed will I establish for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is the seed of David, but that of Abraham.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is the seed of Abraham?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And to thy seed,” He saith, “which is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps that Christ, the Head of the Church, the Saviour of the body, is the seed of Abraham, and therefore of David; but we are not Abraham’s seed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are assuredly; as the Apostle saith, “And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this sense, then, let us take the words, brethren, “Thy seed will I stablish for ever,” not only of that Flesh of Christ, born of the Virgin Mary, but also of all of us who believe in Christ, for we are limbs of that Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This body cannot be deprived of its Head: if the Head is in glory for ever, so are the limbs, so that Christ remains entire for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy seed will I stablish for ever: and set up thy throne to generation and generation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We suppose he saith, “for ever,” because it is “to generation and generation:” since he has said above, with “my mouth will I ever be showing Thy truth to generation and generation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “to generation and generation”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To every generation: for the word needed not as many repetitions, as the coming and passing away of the several generations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The multiplication of generations is signified and set forth to notice by the repetition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are possibly two generations to be understood, as ye are aware, my beloved brethren, and as I have before explained? for there is now a generation of flesh and blood: there will be a future generation in the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ is proclaimed here: He will be proclaimed there: here He is proclaimed, that He may be believed in: there, He will be welcomed, that He may be seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will set up Thy throne from one generation to another.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ hath now a throne in us, His throne is set up in us: for unless he sat enthroned within us, He would not rule us: but if we were not ruled by Him, we should be thrown down by ourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore sits within us, reigning over us: He sits also in another generation, which will come from the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ will reign for ever over His Saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God has promised this; He hath said it: if this is not enough, God hath sworn it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then the promise is certain, not on account of our deservings, but of His pity, no one ought to be afraid in proclaiming that which he cannot doubt of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let that strength then inspire our hearts, whence Æthan received his name, “strong in heart:” let us preach the truth of God, the utterance of God, His promises, His oath; and let us, strengthened on every side by these means, glorify God, and by bearing Him along with us, become Heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, the very Heavens shall praise Thy wondrous works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Heavens will not praise their own merits, but Thy wondrous works, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in every act of mercy on the lost, of justification of the unrighteous, what do we praise but the wondrous works of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou praisest Him, because the dead have risen: praise Him yet more, because the lost are redeemed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What grace, what mercy of God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest a man yesterday a whirlpool of drunkenness, to-day an ornament of sobriety: a man yesterday the sink of luxury, to-day the beauty of temperance: yesterday a blasphemer of God, to-day His praiser: yesterday the slave of the creature, to-day the worshipper of the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From all these desperate states men are thus converted: let them not look at their own merits: let them become Heavens, and praise the wondrous works of Him by whom they were made Heavens.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For who is he among the clouds, who shall be compared unto Thee, Lord!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this to be the praise of the Heavens, is this to be their rain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What? are the preachers confident, because “none among the clouds shall be compared unto the Lord”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Does it appear to you, brethren, a high ground of praise, that the clouds cannot be compared with their Creator?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it is taken in its literal, not in its mystical meaning, it is not so: what? are the stars that are above the clouds to be compared with the Lord? what? can the Sun, Moon, Angels, Heavens, be even compared with the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is it then that he says, as if he meant some high praise, “For who is he among the clouds?” etc. We understand, my brethren, those clouds, as the Heavens, to be the preachers of truth; Prophets, Apostles, the announcers of the word of God.…If therefore the clouds are the preachers of the truth, let us first enquire why they are clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the same men are Heavens and clouds: Heavens from the brightness of the truth, clouds from the hidden things of the flesh: for all clouds are obscure, owing to their mortality: and they come and go.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is on account of these very obscurities of the flesh, that is, of the clouds, that the Apostle saith, “Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who will bring to light the hidden things of darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You see at this moment what a man is saying: but what he has in his heart, you cannot see: what is forced from the cloud, you see, what is kept within the cloud, you see not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whose eyes pierce the cloud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The clouds therefore are the preachers of the truth in the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Creator of all things Himself came in the flesh.…We are called clouds on account of the flesh, and we are preachers of the truth on account of the showers of the clouds: but our flesh comes in one way, His by another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We too are called sons of God, but He is the Son of God in another sense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His cloud comes from a Virgin, He is the Son from eternity, co-eternal with the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is he then among the clouds, that shall be compared unto the Lord? and what is he among the sons of God, that shall be like unto the Lord?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Lord Himself say whether He can find one like unto Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whom do men say that I the Son of Man am?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because I appear, because I am seen, because I walk among you, and perhaps at present I am become common; say, whom do men say that I the Son of Man am?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely when they see a son of man, they see a cloud; but say, “Whom do men say that I am?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In answer they gave Him the reports of men; “Some say that Thou art John the Baptist: some Elias, and others Jeremias, or one of the prophets.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many clouds and sons of God are here mentioned: for because they were righteous and holy, as the sons of God, Jeremias, Elias, and John are called also sons of God: in their character of preachers of God, they are styled clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have said what clouds men imagine Me to be: do ye too say, “Whom say ye that I am?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter replying in behalf of all, one for those who were one, answered, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God;” not like those sons of God who are not made equal to Thee: Thou hast come in the flesh: but not as the clouds, who are not to be compared unto Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8. …“God is very greatly to be feared in the counsel of the righteous, and to be had in dread of all them that are round about Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is everywhere; who therefore are round about Him, who is everywhere?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He has some round about Him, He is represented as finite on every side.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover, if it is truly said to God and of God, “of His greatness there is no end;” who remain, who are round about Him, except because He who is everywhere, chose to be born of the flesh on one spot, to dwell among one nation, in one place to be crucified, from one spot to rise again and ascend into Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where He did this, the Gentiles are round about Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He remained where He did these things, He would not be “great, and be had in dread of all them that are round about Him;” but since He preached when there in such a manner as to send preachers of His own name through all nations over the whole world; by working miracles among His servants, He is become “great, and to be had in dread of all them that are round about Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord God of Hosts, who is like unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy truth, most mighty Lord, is on every side” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is Thy power: Thou hast made Heaven and earth, and all things that in them are: but greater still is thy loving-kindness, which has shown forth Thy truth to all around Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if Thou hadst been preached only on the spot where Thou didst deign to be born, to suffer, to rise again, to ascend; the truth of that promise of God would have been fulfilled, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers: but the promise, “that the Gentiles may glorify God for His mercy,” would not have been fulfilled, had not that truth been explained, and diffused to those around Thee from the spot where Thou didst deign to appear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On that spot Thou didst thunder out of Thy own cloud: but to scatter rain upon the Gentiles round about, Thou hast sent other clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly in Thy power hast Thou fulfilled what Thou hast said, “Hereafter shall ye see the Son of Man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10. …For ye have heard, like men accustomed to the watering of the clouds of God, “Thy truth” then “is in the circuit of Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when without persecutions, when without opposition, since it is said, that “He was born for a sign which shall be spoken against”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then that nation, where Thou didst deign to be born, and to dwell, was as a land separated from the waves of the heathen, so that it appeared dry and ready for watering with rain, while the rest of the nations were as a sea in the bitterness of their sterility; what do Thy preachers who scatter Thy truth in circuit of Thee, when the waves of that sea rage furiously?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou rulest the power of the sea” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what was the result of the sea raging thus, but the day which we are now keeping holy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It slew Martyrs, scattered seeds of blood, the harvest of the Church sprang up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Safely then let the clouds go forth: let them diffuse Thy truth in circuit of Thee, let them not fear the savage waves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou rulest the power of the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sea swells, buffets, and roars: but “God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted beyond what ye are able:” and so, “Thou stillest the waves thereof when they rise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, what hast Thou done in the sea itself, to pacify its rage, and to weaken it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast humbled the proud as one that is wounded” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain proud serpent in the sea, of which another passage of Scripture speaks, “I will command the serpent, and he shall bite him;” and again, “There is that Leviathan, whom Thou hast made to mock him,” whose head He bruises above the water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou,” he says, “hast humbled the proud, as one that is wounded.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast humbled Thyself, and the proud was humbled: for the proud held the proud ones through pride: but the great One is humbled, and by believing in Him become small.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While the little one is nourished by the example of One who from greatness descended to humility, the devil has lost what he held: because the proud held only the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When such an example of humility was displayed before them, men learned to condemn their own pride, and to imitate the humility of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus also the devil, by losing those whom he had in his power, has even himself been humbled; not chastened, but thrown prostrate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast humbled the proud like one that is wounded.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast been humbled, and hast humbled others: Thou hast been wounded, and hast wounded others: for Thy blood, as it was shed to blot the handwriting of sins, could not but wound him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what was the ground of his pride, except the bond which he held against us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This bond, this handwriting, Thou hast blotted out with Thy blood: him therefore hast Thou wounded, from whom Thou hast rescued so many victims.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You must understand the devil wounded, not by the piercing of the flesh, which he has not, but by the bruising of his proud heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast scattered Thine enemies abroad with Thy mighty arm.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heavens are thine, the earth also is Thine” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From Thee, over Thy earth they rain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thine are the heavens, by whom is preached Thy truth in circuit of Thee; “Thine is the earth,” which has received Thy truth in circuit of Thee; and what has resulted from that rain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast laid the foundation of the round world, and all that therein is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast created the north and the seas” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For nothing has any power against Thee, against its Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The world indeed may rage through its own malice, and the perversity of its will; does it nevertheless pass over the bound laid down by the Creator, who made all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then do I fear the north wind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do I fear the seas?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the north indeed is the devil, who said, “I will sit in the sides of the north; I will be like the Most High;” but Thou hast humbled, as one wounded, the proud one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus what Thou hast done in them has more force for Thy dominion, than their own will has for their wickedness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast created the north and the seas.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thabor and Hermon shall rejoice in Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those mountains are here understood, but they have a meaning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thabor and Hermon shall rejoice in Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thabor, when interpreted, signifies an approaching light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence comes the light of which it is said, “Ye are the light of the world,” unless from Him concerning whom it is written, “That was the true light, which lighteth every man coming into the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The light then which is the light of the world comes from that light which is not kindled from any other source, so that there is no fear lest it be extinguished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The light then comes from Him, who is that candle which is not set beneath the bushel, but on a candlestick, Thabor the coming light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hermon means his curse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly the light comes and is made the curse of him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom but the devil, the wounded one, the proud one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our illumination then is given from Thee; that he is held accursed of us, who kept us in his own error and pride, is from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thabor and Hermon, therefore, shall rejoice,” not in their own merits, “but in Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they shall say, “Not unto us, Lord, not unto us, but to Thy name give the praise,” on account of the raging sea: lest “the heathen say, Where is now their God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast a mighty arm” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man arrogate anything to himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast a mighty arm:” by Thee we were created, by Thee we have been defended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast a mighty arm: strong be Thy hand, and high be Thy right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Righteousness and judgment are the preparation of Thy seat” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy righteousness and judgment will appear in the end: they are now hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of Thy righteousness it is treated in another Psalm, “on the hidden things of the Son.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There will then be a manifestation of Thy righteousness and judgment: some will be set on the right, others on the left hand: and the unbelieving will tremble, when they see what now they mock at, and believe not: the righteous will rejoice, when they shall see what they now see not, yet believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Righteousness and judgment are the preparation of Thy seat:” especially in the Day of Judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then now? “mercy and truth go before Thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I should fear the preparation of Thy seat, Thy justice, and Thy coming judgment, did not mercy and truth go before Thee: why should I at the end fear Thy righteousness, when with Thy mercy going before Thee Thou blottest out my sins, and by showing forth Thy truth fulfillest Thy promises?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mercy and truth go before Thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “all the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In all these things shall we not rejoice? or shall we contain our joy? or shall words suffice for our gladness? or shall the tongue be able to express our rejoicing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore no words suffice, “Blessed is the people, O Lord, that knoweth glad shouting” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O blessed people! dost thou conceive aright, dost thou understand, glad shouting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For except thou understand glad shouting, thou canst not be blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do I mean by understanding glad shouting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether thou knowest the source of that rejoicing which is beyond words to express.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this joy is not of thyself, since “he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoice not then in thy own pride, but in God’s grace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See that that grace is such, that the tongue fails to express its greatness, and then thou understandest glad shouting.…O Lord, “they shall walk in the light of Thy countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall rejoice in Thy name all the day” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Thabor and Hermon shall rejoice in Thy name: all day shall they rejoice, if they will, in Thy name; but if they will rejoice in their own name, they shall not rejoice all day: for they shall not continue in their joy, when they shall delight in themselves, and fall through pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they may rejoice all day, therefore, “they shall rejoice in Thy name, and in Thy righteousness shall they be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in their own, but in Thine: lest they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some are noted by the Apostle, that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge, “being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own,” and not rejoicing in Thy light, and thus “not submitting themselves unto the righteousness of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why? because “they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the people who knoweth glad shouting (for the former err from want of knowledge, but blessed is the people not that knoweth not, but that knoweth glad shouting), whence ought it to shout, whence to rejoice, but in Thy name, walking in the light of Thy countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it shall deserve to be exalted, but in Thy righteousness: let every man take away altogether his own righteousness, and be trembled: the righteousness of God shall come, and he shall be exalted, “and in Thy righteousness shall they be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou art the glory of their strength: and in Thy good pleasure Thou shalt lift up our horns” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17): because it has seemed good to Thee, not because we are worthy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For of the Lord is our taking up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I was moved like a heap of sand, that I might fall; and I should have fallen, had not the Lord taken me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For of the Lord is (our) taking up: and of the Holy One of Israel our King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself is thy taking up, Himself thy illumination: in His light thou art safe, in His light thou walkest, in His righteousness thou art exalted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He took thee up, He guards thy weakness: He gives thee strength of Himself, not of thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou spakest sometime in vision unto Thy sons, and saidst” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou spakest in thy vision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst reveal this to Thy Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason Thou spakest in vision, that is, in revelation: whence Prophets were called seers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They saw something within, which they were to speak without: and secretly they heard what they preached openly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then “Thou spakest in vision unto Thy sons, and saidst, I have laid help upon One that is mighty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye understand Who is meant by mighty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have exalted One chosen out of the people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Who is meant by chosen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One who, ye rejoice, is already exalted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have found David My servant:” that David from David’s seed: “with My holy oil have I anointed Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 20): for it is said of Him, “God, even Thy God, hath anointed Thee with the oil of gladness above Thy fellows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My hand shall hold Him fast, and My arm shall strengthen Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 21): because there was a taking up of man; because flesh was assumed in the Virgin’s womb, because by Him who in the form of God is coequal with the Father, the form of a servant was taken, and He became obedient unto death, even the death of the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The enemy shall not be able to do him violence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The enemy rages indeed but he shall not be able to do Him violence: he is wont to hurt, but he shall not hurt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then shall he afflict Him? he will exercise Him, but he shall not hurt Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall be profit in his raging; for those against whom he rages shall be crowned in their conquering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how is he conquered, if he rages not against us? or where is God our helper, if we fight not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The enemy therefore shall do what is in his power; but “he shall not be able to do Him violence: the son of wickedness shall not come nigh to hurt Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will cut in pieces His enemies before His face” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are cut in pieces from their conspiracy, and in that they believe they are cut in pieces; for they believe by degrees; as when the calf’s head was ground small, they will come to be the drink of God’s people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Moses ground down the calf’s head, and sprinkled it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the unbelieving are ground: they believe by degrees; and they are drunk by the people of God, and pass into Christ’s body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will cut in pieces His foes before His face: and put to flight them that hate Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My truth also and My mercy is with Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember, as much as ye can, how often these two attributes are urged upon us, that we render them back to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as He showed us mercy that He might blot out our sins, and truth in fulfilling His promises; so also we, walking in His path, ought to give back to Him mercy and truth; mercy, in pitying the wretched; truth, in not judging unjustly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not truth rob you of mercy, nor mercy hinder truth: for if through mercy you shall have judged contrary to truth, or by rigorous truth shall have forgotten mercy, you will not be walking in the path of God, where “mercy and truth meet together.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in My name shall His horn be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should I say more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye are Christians, recognise Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will set His hand also in the sea” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 25): that is, He shall rule over the Gentiles; “and His right hand in the floods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rivers run into the sea: avaricious men roll onwards into the bitterness of this world: yet all these kinds of men will be subject to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall call me, Thou art My Father, and the lifter up of My salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will make Him my first-born; higher than the kings of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Martyrs, whose birthdays we are celebrating, shed their blood on account of these things, which were believed though not yet seen; how much more brave ought we to be, as we see what they believed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they had not yet seen Christ raised on high among the kings of the earth: as yet princes were taking counsel together against the Lord and His Anointed: what follows in the same Psalm was not then fulfilled, “Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be learned, ye that are judges of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now indeed Christ has been exalted among the kings of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My mercy will I keep for Him for ever: and my Testament faithful with Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On His account, the Testament is faithful: in Him the Testament is mediated: He is the Sealer, the Mediator of the Testament, the Surety of the Testament, the Witness of the Testament, the Heritage of the Testament, the Coheir of the Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His seed will I make to endure world without end” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only for this world, but unto the world without end: whither His seed, which is His heritage, the seed of Abraham, which is Christ, will pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if ye are Christ’s, ye are also Abraham’s seed: and if ye are destined His heirs for ever, “He will establish His seed unto world without end: and His throne as the days of Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The thrones of earthly kings are as the days of the earth: different are the days of Heaven from those of earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The days of Heaven are those years of which it is said, “Thou art the same, and Thy years shall not fail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The days of the earth are soon overtaken by their successors: those which precede are shut out from us: nor do those which succeed remain: but they come that they may go, and are almost gone before they are come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are the days of earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the days of Heaven, which are also the “One day” of Heaven, and the never failing years, have neither beginning nor end: nor is any day there narrowed between yesterday and to-morrow: no one there expects the future, nor loses the past: but the days of Heaven are always present, where His throne shall be for ever and ever.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a strong pledge of the promise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sons of this David are the children of the Bridegroom; all Christians therefore are called His sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is much indeed that God promises, that if Christians, that is, “If his children forsake My law, and walk not in My judgments” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 30); “if they profane My statutes, and keep not My commandments” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 31); I will not spurn them, nor will I send them away from Me in perdition: but what will I do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will visit their offences with the rod, and their sin with scourges” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not the mercy of one that calls them only; but also that chastises and scourges them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let therefore thy Father’s hand be upon thee, and if thou art a good son, repel not chastening; for “what son is there, to whom his father giveth not chastening?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him chasten him, so long as He takes not from him His mercy: let Him beat him when obstinate, as long as He does not disinherit him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hast well understood the promises of thy Father, fear not to be scourged, but to be disinherited: “for whom the Lord loveth He chasteneth: and scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Does the sinful son spurn chastening, when he sees the only Son without sin scourged?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will visit their offences with the rod.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus too the Apostle threatens: “What will ye? shall I come unto you with a rod?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not pious sons say, if Thou art coming with a rod, come not at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is better to be taught with the Father’s rod, than to perish in the caresses of the robber.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nevertheless, My mercy will I not utterly take from Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From that David to whom I gave these promises, whom “I anointed with my holy oil of gladness above His fellows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do you recognise Him from whom God will not utterly take away His mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That no one may anxiously say, since He speaks of Christ as Him from whom He will not take away His mercy, What then will become of the sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He say anything like this, “I will not take My loving-kindness utterly from them”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will visit,” He saith, “their offences with the rod, and their sin with scourges.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst expect for thy own security, “I will not utterly take my loving-kindness from” them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed this is the reading of some books, but not of the most accurate: though, where they have it, it is a reading by no means inconsistent with the real meaning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how can it be said that He will not utterly take His mercy from Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Has the Saviour of the body committed aught of sin either in Heaven or in earth, “who sitteth even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet it is from Christ: but from His members, His body which is the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in this sense He speaks of it as a great thing that He will not take away His mercies from Him, supposing us not to recognise the only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father; for there the Man is not counted for His Person, but the One Person is God and Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore does not utterly take His mercies from Him, when He takes not His mercy from His body, His members, in which, even while He was enthroned in Heaven, He was still suffering persecutions on earth; and when He cried from Heaven, “Saul, Saul,” not why persecutest thou My servants, nor why persecutest thou My saints, nor My disciples, but, “why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then, while no one persecuted Him when sitting in Heaven, He cried out, “Why persecutest thou Me?” when the Head recognised its limbs, and His love allowed not the Head to separate Himself from the union of the body: so, when He taketh not away His mercies from Him, it is surely that He taketh it not from us, who are His limbs and body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet ought we not on that account to sin not without apprehension, and perversely to assure ourselves that we shall not perish, be our actions what they may.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are certain sins and certain offences, to define and discourse of which it is either impossible for me, or if it were possible, it would be too tedious for the time we have at present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no man can say that he is without sin; for if he says so, he will lie; “if we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one therefore is needfully scourged for his own sins; but the mercy of God is not taken away from him, if he be a Christian.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly if thou committest such offences as to repel the hand of Him who chasteneth, the rod of Him who scourgeth thee, and art angry at the correction of God, and fliest from thy Father when He chasteneth thee, and wilt not suffer Him to be thy Father, because He spares thee not when thou dost sin; thou hast estranged thyself from thy heritage, He has not thrown thee off; for if thou wouldest abide being scourged, thou wouldest not abide disinherited.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor will I do hurt in My truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For His mercy in setting free shall not be taken away, lest His truth in taking vengeance do harm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My covenant will I not profane, nor reject the thing that is gone out of my lips” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because his sons sin, I will not on this account be found false: I have promised; I will do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose they choose to sin even as past hope, and so fall into sins as to offend their Father’s countenance, and deserve to be disinherited; is it not still God Himself, of whom it is said, “From these stones” He “will raise up sons to Abraham”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore I tell you, brethren, many Christians sin venially, many are scourged and so corrected for their sin, chastened, and cured; many turn away altogether, striving with a stiff neck against the discipline of the Father, even wholly refusing God as their Father, though they have the mark of Christ, and so fall into such sins, that it can only be announced against them, “that they who do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, Christ shall not be destitute of an inheritance on their account: not for the chaff’s sake shall the wheat also perish: nor on account of bad fish shall nothing be cast into the vessels from that net.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord knows them that are His.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who predestined us before we were born, promised undoubtingly: “For whom He did predestinate, them He also called: and whom He called, them He also justified: and whom He justified, them He also glorified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let desperate sinners sin as far as they choose: let the members of Christ reply, “If God is with us, who shall be against us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God will not therefore do hurt in His truth, nor will He “profane His Testament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Testament remains immovable, because in His foreknowledge He predestined His heirs; and “He will not reject the thing that is gone out of His lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen for thy confirmation in hope, for thy security, if thou knowest thyself to be among the members of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have sworn once by My holiness that I will not lie unto David” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wait till God swear a second time?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How often is He to swear, if in one oath He is false?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One oath He made for our life, who sent His Only One to die for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have sworn once by My holiness, that I will not lie unto David.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His seed shall endure for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His seed endures for ever; because the Lord knows them that are His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His seat is like as the sun before me:” “and as the moon perfect for evermore: and the faithful witness in heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are His seat, in whom He sits and reigns.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if His seat, His members also; because even our members are the seat of our head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how all our other members sustain our head: but the head supports nothing above itself, but is itself supported by the rest of our limbs, as if the whole body of a man were the seat of his head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His seat, therefore, all in whom God reigns, “shall be like as the sun before Me,” He saith: because the righteous in the kingdom of My Father “shall shine like the sun.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the sun is meant in a spiritual, not a bodily sense, as that which shines from Heaven, which He maketh to rise upon the just and unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, that sun is not before men’s eyes only, but even those of cattle and the smallest insects; for which of the vilest animals sees not that sun?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does he say to distinguish the sun meant here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like as the sun before Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not before men, before the flesh, before mortal animals, but “before Me, and as the moon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what moon? one “that is perfect for evermore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For although that moon which we know becomes perfect, the next day she begins to wane, after her orb is full.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall be as the moon perfect for evermore,” He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His seat shall be made perfect as the moon, but that moon is one which will be perfect for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If as the sun, why also as the moon? the Scriptures usually signify by the moon the mortality of this flesh, because of its increasings and decreasings, because of its transitory nature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The moon is also interpreted as Jericho: one who was descending from Jerusalem to Jericho fell among robbers: for he was descending from immortality to mortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Similar then is the flesh to that moon, which every month suffers increase and decrease: but that flesh of ours will be perfect in the resurrection: “and a faithful witness in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then, if it was our mind only that would be perfected, he would compare us only to the sun: if our body only, to the moon; but as God will perfect us in both, in respect of the mind it is said, “like as the sun before Me,” because God only seeth the mind: and “as the moon,” so is the flesh: which “shall be made perfect for evermore,” in the resurrection of the dead: “and a faithful witness in Heaven,” because all that was asserted of the resurrection of the dead was true.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I beseech you, hear this again more clearly, and remember it: for I know that some understand, while others are yet enquiring perhaps what I meant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no article of the Christian faith which has encountered such contradiction as that of the resurrection of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, He who was born for a sign that should be spoken against, resumed His own flesh after death to meet the caviller; and He who could have so completely cured His wounds that their scars would have entirely vanished, retained those scars in His body, that He might cure the wounds of doubt in the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Indeed nothing has been attacked with the same pertinacious, contentious contradiction, in the Christian faith, as the resurrection of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the immortality of the soul many Gentile philosophers have disputed at great length, and in many books they have left it written that the soul is immortal: when they come to the resurrection of the flesh, they doubt not indeed, but they most openly deny it, declaring it to be absolutely impossible that this earthly flesh can ascend to Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus that moon shall be perfect for evermore, and shall be the faithful witness in heaven against all gain-sayers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These promises, so sure, so firm, so open, so unquestioned, were made concerning Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For although some are mysteriously veiled, yet some are so clear, that all that is obscure is easily revealed by them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such being the case, see what follows: “But Thou hast approved and brought to nothing and forsaken Thine Anointed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast overthrown the testament of Thy servant, and profaned His holiness on the ground” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast broken down all His hedges, and made His strongholds a terror” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 40).…How is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast promised all those things: and Thou hast brought to pass their reverse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where are now the promises which but a little before filled us with delight? which we so joyfully applauded, which we so fearlessly made our boast of?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is as if one promised, and another destroyed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is the mystery: for the words are not “another,” but “Thou,” Thou who didst promise, who didst even swear in condescension to human doubt, Thou hast promised this, and done thus!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall I get Thy oath, where shall I find Thy promise fulfilled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would then God promise, or swear thus falsely? and yet why then these promises, and these acts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I answer, that He acted thus in fulfilment of those promises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who am I, to say this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see therefore whether it is the language of the Truth; what I say will not then be without foundation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was David to whom the fulfilment of these promises in his seed, that is, in Christ, was promised: and as they were addressed to David, men expected their completion in David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Further, lest when any Christian asserted these promises to have referred to Christ, another by applying them to David, because he described the fulfilment of all of them in David, might thus err; He cancelled them in David, thus obliging us when we see them unfulfilled in David, to look to another quarter for their fulfilment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus also in the case of Esau and Jacob, we find the elder worshipped by the younger, though it is written, “The elder shall serve the younger;” so when you see it unfulfilled in those two brothers, you look for two peoples in whom to discover the completion of what God in His truth deigns to promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the fruit of thy body,” saith the Lord unto David, “shall I set upon thy sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He promised from his seed something for evermore: and, Solomon, born to him, became master of such wisdom, that the promise of God respecting the fruit of David’s body was believed to have been fulfilled in him; but Solomon fell, and gave room for hoping for Christ; that since God can neither be deceived nor deceive, He might not make His promise to rest in one who He knew would fall, but you might after the fall of Solomon look back to God, and demand His promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast Thou, O Lord, deceived?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast Thou failed to fulfil Thy promise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost Thou not exhibit what Thou hast sworn?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps God might reply, I swore and promised: but Solomon would not persevere.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst not Thou, Lord God, know beforehand that he would not persevere?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Indeed Thou didst know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then didst Thou promise me what should be eternal in one who would not persevere?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast Thou not answered; “But if his children forsake My law, and walk not in My judgments; if they keep not My statutes, and profane My testament;” yet My promise shall remain, and My oath shall be fulfilled: “I have sworn once in My Holiness,” within, in a certain mystery, in the very spring whence the Prophets drank, whence they burst forth to us of these things, “I have sworn once” that I will not fail David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Show forth then what Thou hast sworn, give us what Thou hast promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fulfilment is taken from that David, that it might not be looked for in that David: wait therefore for what I have promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even David himself knew this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider his words; “Thou hast rejected and brought him down to nothing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is Thy promise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast put off Thine anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This expression cheers us, among much that is sorrowful: for the promise of God is still valid; for Thou hast put off Thine Anointed, not taken Him away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See then what was the fate of that David, in whom the ignorant hoped for the fulfilment of the promises of God, in order that those promises might be more firmly relied upon for their fulfilment in another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast put off Thine Anointed: Thou hast overthrown the testament of Thy servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For where is the Old Testament of the Jews? where that land of promise, in which they sinned while they dwelt in it, on the overthrow of which they wandered afar?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ask you for the kingdom of the Jews; it exists not: you ask for the altar of the Jews; it is not: you ask for the sacrifice of the Jews; it is not: you ask for the priesthood of the Jews; it is not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast overthrown the testament of Thy servant, and profaned his holiness on the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast shown that what they thought holy, was earthly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast broken down all his hedges,” with which Thou hast entrenched him: for how could he have been spoiled unless his hedges had been broken down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast made his strongholds a terror.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That it should be said to the sinners, “For if God spared not the natural branches, take heed lest He also spare not thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All they that go by the way have spoiled him:” that is, all the heathen that go by the way, meaning, all who pass through this life, have spoiled Israel, have spoiled David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First of all, see his fragments in all nations: for it is of the Jews that it is said, “They shall be a portion for foxes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Scripture calls wicked, crafty, and cowardly kings, whom another’s virtue terrifies, foxes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus when our Lord Himself was speaking of the threatening Herod, He said, “Go ye, and tell that fox.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The king who fears no man, is not a fox: like that Lion of Judah, of whom it is said, “Stooping down Thou didst rise up, and didst sleep as a lion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At Thy will Thou didst stoop down, at Thy will didst rise; because Thou wouldest, Thou didst sleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thus in another Psalm he says, “I slept.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was not the sentence complete, “I slept, and took rest, and rose up again, because the Lord shall uphold Me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is the word ego added? and thus with a strong emphasis on the word I, they raged against Me, they troubled Me: but had I not willed, I had not slept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those then concerning whom it was declared that they should be a portion for foxes, are now spoken of as follows; “All they that go by have spoiled him: and he is become a reproach to his neighbours” (ver.

verses.text: “Thou hast set up the right hand of his enemies, and made all his adversaries to rejoice” (ver.

verses.text: Look at the Jews, and see all things fulfilled that were predicted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Naturally then is he conquered, naturally taken prisoner, naturally made an outcast from his kingdom, naturally scattered abroad: for he lost that land, for which he slew the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast loosed him from cleansing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amongst all the evils, this is a matter for great fear; for howsoever God may beat, howsoever He may be wroth, howsoever He may flog and scourge, yet let Him scourge him bound, whom He is to cleanse, not “loose him from cleansing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He loose him from being purified, he becomes incapable of cleansing, and must be an outcast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From what cleansing then is the Jew loosed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From faith; for by faith we live: and it is said of faith, “purifying their hearts by faith:” and as it is only the faith of Christ that cleanses; by disbelief in Christ, they are loosed from purification.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast loosed him from cleansing, and cast his throne down to the ground.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so Thou hast broken it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The days of his seat hast Thou shortened” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They imagined that they should reign for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And covered him with confusion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things happened to the Jews, Christ yet not being taken away, but His advent deferred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore see whether God fulfils His promises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After these stern penalties which have been recorded as having been inflicted upon this people and kingdom, that God might not be supposed to have fulfilled His promises in it, and so not to grant another kingdom in Christ, of which kingdom there shall be no end; the Prophet addresses Him in these words, “Lord, how long wilt Thou hide Thyself unto the end?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For possibly it was not from them and to the end; because “blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in, and so all Israel shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the mean while “shall Thy wrath burn like fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O remember what my substance is” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That David, who was placed among the Jews in the flesh, in Christ in hope, speaks “Remember what is my substance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not because the Jews fell away, did my substance fail: for from that people came the Virgin Mary, and from her the flesh of Christ; that Flesh sins not, but purifies sins; there, saith David, is my substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O remember what my substance is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the root has not entirely perished; the seed shall come to whom the promise was made, ordained by Angels in the hand of a Mediator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast not made all the sons of men for nought” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo! all the sons of men have gone into vanity: yet Thou hast not made them for nought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then all went into vanity, whom Thou hast not made for nought; hast Thou not reserved some instrument to purify them from vanity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This which Thou hast reserved to Thyself to cleanse men from vanity is Thy Holy One, in Him is my substance: for from Him are all, whom Thou hast not made for nought, purified from their own vanity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To them it is said, “O ye sons of men, how long are ye heavy in heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore have ye such pleasure in vanity, and seek after leasing?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps they might become anxious, and turn from their vanity, and when they found themselves polluted with it, might seek for purification from it: then help them, make them secure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Know this also, that the Lord hath made wonderful His Holy One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has made His Holy One to be admired: thence He has purified all from their vanity: there, saith David, is my substance: O remember it!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast not made all the sons of men for nought.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast therefore reserved something to purify them: and who is He whom Thou hast reserved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What man is he that liveth, and shall not see death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man then who shall live and not see death, shall purify them from nothingness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He made not all men for nought, nor can He who made them so despise His own creatures, as not to convert and purify them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What man is he that shall live, and shall not see death?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For being raised from the dead He dieth no more, and death hath no more dominion over Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as in another Psalm it is said, “Thou shalt not leave my soul in Hell, neither shalt Thou suffer Thy Holy One to see corruption,” the Apostolic teaching takes up this testimony, and in the Acts of the Apostles thus argues against the unbelieving; Men and brethren, we know that the patriarch David is dead and buried, and his flesh hath seen corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it cannot be said of him, “neither shalt Thou suffer Thy Holy One to see corruption.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom then is it said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What man is he that shall live, and shall not see death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps there is no man such.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but “who is it?” is said to make thee inquire, not despair.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps there may be some man “that shall live, and shall not see death,” and yet perhaps he did not speak of Christ, who died?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no man “that shall live, and shall not see death,” except Him who died for mortals.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thou mayest be assured that it is said of Him, consider the sequel; “What man is he that liveth, and shall not see death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He never die then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then shall He live, and never see death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall deliver His own soul from the hands of Hell.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is spoken of alone indeed, in that He alone of all others “shall live, and shall not see death: He shall deliver His own soul from the hand of Hell,” because although the rest of His faithful shall rise from the dead, and shall themselves live for evermore, without seeing death; yet they shall not themselves deliver their own souls from the hands of Hell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who delivers His own soul from the hands of Hell, Himself delivers those of His believers: they cannot do so of themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Prove that He delivers His own soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No man taketh ‘it from Me;’ for I Myself slept, but I lay it down of Myself, and take it again,” because it is He Himself who delivers His own soul from the hands of Hell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the very faith in Christ great difficulties occurred, and the heathen in their rage long said, “When shall he die, and his name perish?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On account of these then who have now long believed in Christ, but were destined to doubt for some time, these words follow, “Lord, where are Thy old loving-kindnesses?” (ver.

verses.text: We have now acknowledged Christ our purifier, we now possess Him in whom Thy promises were to be fulfilled; show forth in Him what Thou hast promised.

verses.text: It is He Himself that shall live, and not see death: Himself who delivers His own soul from the hand of Hell: and yet we are still in suffering.

verses.text: Thus spoke the Martyrs, whose birthdays we are celebrating.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shall live, and not see death: He delivers His soul from the hands of Hell: yet “for Thy sake we are killed all the day long: and are counted as sheep appointed to be slain.”

verses.text: “Lord, where are Thy old loving-kindnesses which Thou swarest unto David in Thy truth?”
