verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even while Christ was living, and while He was sitting on His Father’s right hand, reproaches were cast against the Christians: they long were reproached with the name of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That widowed one who brought forth, and whose children were more than those of the married wife, heard ill names, heard reproaches: but the Church, multiplied as she is, extending right and left, no longer remembers the reproach of her widowhood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember, Lord,” in the memory of whom there is abundant sweetness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember,” forget not.

verses.text: “Being defamed we entreat: we are made as the filth of the earth, and are the offscouring of all things unto this day.”

verses.text: Long the Christians bore reproaches in their bosom, in their heart: nor dared resist their revilers; before, when it was a crime to answer a heathen: it is now a crime to remain a heathen.

verses.text: Thanks be to the Lord!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is styled “dead” on account of the three days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherewith then have thine enemies blasphemed Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not with the loss, not with the perdition of Thine Anointed, but with His “change.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was changed from temporal to eternal life: He was changed from the Jews to the Gentiles; He was changed from earth to heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then Thy vain enemies blaspheme Thee still for the change of Thine Anointed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would that they may be changed: they will not in that case blaspheme the change of Christ, which displeases them since they themselves will not be changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there is no change with them, and they fear not God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have blasphemed the change of Christ; but what dost thou answer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The blessing of the Lord for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amen and Amen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to His mercy, thanks to His grace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We express our thanks: we do not give them, nor return them, nor repay them: we express our thanks in words, while in fact we retain our sense of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saved us for no reward, He heeded not our impieties: He searched us out when we searched not for Him, He found, redeemed, emancipated us from the bondage of the devil and the power of his wicked angels: He drew us to Him to purify us by that faith, from which He releases those enemies only who believe not, and who for that reason cannot be purified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those who still remain infidels say every day what they choose; day by day they shall be fewer and fewer that remain; let them revile, mock, accuse, not the death, but the change of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do they not see that, when they say these things, they fail in purpose either by believing or by dying?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For their curse is temporal: but the blessing of the Lord “for evermore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To confirm that blessing is added, “Amen and Amen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the signature of the bond of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Secure then of His promises, let us believe the past, recognise the present, hope for the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the enemy lead us astray from the way, that He, who gathers us like chickens under His wings, may foster us: lest we stray from His wings, and the hawk of the air carry us off while yet unfledged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Christian ought not to hope in himself: if he hopes to be strong, let him be reared by his mother’s warmth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the hen who gathers her young together; whence is the reproach of our Saviour against the unbelieving Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, your house shall be left unto you desolate.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence was it said, “Thou hast made his strongholds a terror.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then they would not be gathered together under the wings of this hen, and have given as a warning to teach us to dread the unclean spirits that fly in the air, seeking daily what they may devour; let us gather ourselves under the wings of this hen, the divine Wisdom, since she is weakened even unto death of her chickens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us love our Lord God, let us love His Church: Him as a Father, Her as a Mother: Him as a Lord, Her as His Handmaid, as we are ourselves the Handmaid’s sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this marriage is held together by a bond of great love: no man offends the one, and wins favour of the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man say, “I go indeed to the idols, I consult possessed ones and fortune-tellers: yet I abandon not God’s Church; I am a Catholic.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While thou holdest to thy Mother, thou hast offended thy Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another says, Far be it from me; I consult no sorcerer, I seek out no possessed one, I never ask advice by sacrilegious divination, I go not to worship idols, I bow not before stones; though I am in the party of Donatus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does it profit you not to have offended your Father, if he avenges your offended Mother? what does it serve you, if you acknowledge the Lord, honour God, preach His name, acknowledge His Son, confess that He sitteth by His right hand; while you blaspheme His Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Does not the analogy of human marriages convince you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose you have some patron, whom you court every day, whose threshold you wear with your visits, whom you daily not only salute, but even worship, to whom you pay the most loyal courtesy; if you utter one calumny against his wife, could you re-enter his house?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hold then, most beloved, hold all with one mind to God the Father, and the Church our Mother.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Celebrate with temperance the birthdays of the Saints, that we may imitate those who have gone before us, and that they who pray for you may rejoice over you; that “the blessing of the Lord may abide on you for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amen and Amen.”
